step_by_step_thinking:
To compare continuous variables such as birth weight (which is measured continuously and represented in grams) across two categorical groups defined by binary exposure status (maternal iron supplement intake Yes or No), an unpaired or independent t-test is recommended. Continuous variables require parametric tests like the t-test, where normal distribution can reasonably assumed unless proven otherwise. Hence,
\boxed{\text{B}} To evaluate the association between maternal iron supplementation and neonatal birth weight, the appropriate statistical test here involves comparing means of a continuous variable (birth weight) across two independent groups (iron supplemented vs. unsupplemented mothers). Since the data comes from distinct and unrelated samples, an **unpaired** or **independent t-test** fits well for assessing the mean difference in birth weights.
